skip_dialog.title: Skip setup?
skip_dialog.description: You can complete setup at any time from Settings. Some features may not work until configured.

welcome.title: Welcome to Macroscope
welcome.subtitle: Let's get you set up in 4 quick steps.

see_what_gets_scanned.title: See what gets scanned
see_what_gets_scanned.desc: Learn which macOS locations Macroscope always audits

pick_an_ai_provider.title: Pick an AI provider
pick_an_ai_provider.desc: Choose Gemini, Claude, OpenAI, or run locally with Ollama

grant_permissions.title: Grant permissions
grant_permissions.desc: macOS will ask for access to specific folders

confirm_project_locations.title: Confirm project locations
confirm_project_locations.desc: Tell Macroscope where your code lives

scan_scope.fixed_note: These macOS locations are audited on every snapshot — this is fixed by design and cannot be disabled.
scan_scope.optional_note: Also optional: In a later step you can add directories where your projects live. Macroscope will then surface large build artifacts (node_modules, .venv, target/) inside them.

ai_provider.hint: You can change this later in Settings.

permissions.hint: You can grant these later — Macroscope will prompt when needed.
permissions.helper_granular: Macroscope only requests the folders it needs.
permissions.helper_fda: One permission grants access to your entire home directory.

automation.title: System Events automation
automation.description: Needed for app inventory via AppleScript

desktop.title: Desktop folder
desktop.description: Access to files on your Desktop

fda.title: Full Disk Access
fda.description: Grants read access to your entire home directory

project_roots.hint: Add directories where your projects live. Macroscope surfaces large build artifacts (node_modules, .venv, target/) inside them.

done.title: You're all set
done.subtitle: Macroscope is ready to take your first snapshot.
